mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS (0.08% as of Aug-2013) compresses the equity risk premium by lowering the discount rate applied to future cash flows, mechanically supporting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the rate remains near the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% Aug-2013, unchanged for multiple years
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or SPX posts negative 60d returns in more than 45% of near-zero-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 7.3% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, investors reprice the probability of imminent Fed asset-purchase tapering, raising discount-rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as the market digests reduced policy accommodation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.3% while FEDFUNDS stays under 0.25%
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following UNRATE crossings below the threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS trend.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS growth and rising CPIAUCSL signals overheating labor demand feeding into inflation, jointly increasing the probability of Fed tightening and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone captures this tightening-expectation channel, only their joint co-movement does.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain exceeds 175k jobs while CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rises simultaneously
regime_hint: labor market tightening ahead of policy normalization
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-CPIAUCSL acceleration signal shows Rank IC below 0.03 versus forward 20d returns over any 2-year window, or single-series PAYEMS-only model performs equally well, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-financial-crisis housing recovery regime, rising HOUST (housing starts, 880k units as of Jul-2013 revised) signals strengthening household balance sheets and construction-linked employment, which propagates into broader consumer demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST level or 3-month trend shows Rank IC below 0.03 against forward 60d SPX returns during the housing recovery window (2012-2015), or reverses sign outside recession-recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in INDPRO (industrial production index, 99.08 as of Jul-2013) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, which is priced into equities with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 3-month INDPRO growth rate shows Rank IC below 0.03 against forward 60d SPX returns over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign flips during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales, $409,691M revised Aug-2013) month-over-month growth above 0.5% signals resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following RSAFS growth triggers above threshold are negative in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp crossing of UNRATE below 7.0% triggers an immediate short-horizon repricing of Fed taper timing expectations, causing a rapid negative S&P 500 reaction within one trading week as markets react to the specific policy-relevant threshold repeatedly cited by FOMC communications.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 7.0% for the first time in the cycle
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns after UNRATE threshold crossings are positive in more than 55% of instances, or no statistically significant reaction is observed (p > 0.05) around the crossing date.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle expectation regime, the joint behavior of rising CPIAUCSL inflation alongside stable-to-rising FEDFUNDS erodes real returns and signals an approaching end to accommodative policy, jointly producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rises above 2% while FEDFUNDS begins rising from near-zero
failure_condition: Joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS rising signal shows Rank IC below 0.03 versus forward 60d SPX returns over any 3-year window, or the combined signal underperforms either single-series model in walk-forward tests.
